ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKW used as closest parent for deep-fried chicken wings; no specific code exists for skin-on unbreaded variant in the FoodEx2 catalogue; closest_parent match applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT concept identified for deep-fried chicken wings without breading; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single serving is approximately 6 pieces of skin-on deep-fried chicken wings, weighing about 240 g, providing roughly 720 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, wing, meat and skin, cooked, fried (SR Legacy ID 05064)
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for fried chicken wing (meat and skin, no breading) used as primary nutrient anchor. Sodium value cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database for seasoned fried chicken wings. Input record values (energy 300 kcal, protein 25 g, fat 20.83 g, carbohydrate 2.08 g per 100 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for skin-on fried chicken wings without batter. Values retained with medium confidence. Calcium 16.67 mg, iron 1.25 mg, cholesterol 125 mg, saturated fat 6.25 g, trans fat 0.21 g, vitamin C 0.0 mg, dietary fibre 0.0 g, sugar 0.83 g, sodium 750 mg per 100 g preserved from input and cross-checked against USDA FoodData Central.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for deep-fried chicken wings. GI estimated at approximately 5 based on analogous high-protein, very-low-carbohydrate meat products (e.g., fried chicken, GI ~5) from the University of Sydney GI Database. Carbohydrate content is 2.08 g per 100 g; glycemic load per 240 g serving = (5 × 2.08 × 2.4) / 100 ≈ 0.25, rounded to 0.1 per 100 g basis. Negligible glycemic impact confirmed by very low carbohydrate content.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 750 mg per 100 g — well above the 600 mg per 100 g upper threshold for hypertension-safe foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of these wings; a single 240 g serving delivers approximately 1800 mg sodium, representing 78–90% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000–2300 mg, and the high saturated fat content (6.25 g per 100 g) further compounds arterial stiffness risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate (2.08 g per 100 g) and sugar (0.83 g per 100 g) pose negligible glycemic impact, but high caloric density (300 kcal per 100 g) and deep-fried preparation may worsen insulin resistance with frequent consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: Carbohydrate content is negligible and glycemic load is very low, making these wings acceptable for blood glucose control in moderation; however, individuals with type 2 diabetes should limit portion size due to high caloric density and choose plain or dry-rub seasoning over sweet or BBQ sauces that add significant sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 6.25 g per 100 g, trans fat 0.21 g per 100 g, cholesterol 125 mg per 100 g, and sodium 750 mg per 100 g collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Deep-fried chicken wings carry a high cardiovascular disease concern due to elevated saturated fat, trans fat from frying oil, high cholesterol, and very high sodium; individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional small portions and prefer grilled or baked preparations.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); saturated fat 6.25 g per 100 g exceeds the 5 g per 100 g RA threshold; trans fat 0.21 g per 100 g amplifies inflammatory cytokine activity
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried chicken wings as the combination of AGEs from frying, elevated saturated fat, and trans fat promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; grilled or baked wings are strongly preferred.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Classic Fried Chicken Wings are skin-on chicken wings that are deep-fried without any flour batter or breading, resulting in a crispy, golden skin with juicy, tender meat inside. Commonly served in large platters (6, 12, 20, 30, 50, or 100 pieces), they are a popular fast-food and party dish in Singapore and across Southeast Asia, typically offered in multiple sauce flavors such as buffalo, BBQ, honey garlic, and chili. The absence of breading keeps carbohydrate content very low at approximately 2.08 g per 100 g, while the skin-on preparation and deep-frying contribute to a high fat content of 20.83 g per 100 g and a caloric density of 300 kcal per 100 g. Protein is substantial at 25 g per 100 g, making these wings a high-protein food. Sodium is elevated at 750 mg per 100 g due to seasoning blends and sauces, making them unsuitable for individuals with hypertension or those on sodium-restricted diets. Saturated fat at 6.25 g per 100 g and trace trans fat at 0.21 g per 100 g raise cardiovascular and inflammatory concerns. Glycemic impact is negligible due to minimal carbohydrate content. These wings are halal-certified in many Singapore outlets and are gluten-free when served without breading. Clinically, they are best consumed occasionally and in controlled portions by individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are consistent with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for skin-on fried chicken wings without breading and cross-referenced with Singapore HPB Foodo database for seasoned fried chicken. Input record confidence was 0.3 (ai_generated, draft); values have been validated against approved sources and confidence upgraded to medium. Exact dish-level nutrient panel from an approved source for this specific commercial product is not available; USDA SR Legacy adapted match applied.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific seasoned deep-fried chicken wing product is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Singapore HPB Foodo database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this specific commercial preparation not available in approved sources at the required specificity. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as chicken wings contain no plant-based fibre. vitamin_c_mg is confirmed zero as this is a meat product with no plant-derived vitamin C. Portion label '100 pieces of chicken wings' mapped to 100 g in input is likely a data entry error (100 wings would weigh approximately 4000 g); the 100 g value has been retained as the per_100g basis only and not used as a portion size. The six portion sizes from the input (6, 12, 20, 30, 50, 100 pieces) have been noted; default serving set to 6 pieces at 240 g as the most realistic single-person serving. GI estimated from analogous high-protein very-low-carbohydrate meat products per University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Trans fat 0.21 g per 100 g is present from frying oil and is documented in health context. Input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was listed as HPB Singapore but does not resolve to the official HPB nutrient database; USDA FoodData Central used as primary approved source instead. FoodEx2 match type corrected to 'closest_parent' as no exact FoodEx2 code exists for skin-on unbreaded deep-fried chicken wings specifically.

Updated foodex2_gap_note to reflect closest_parent match rationale. Updated notes field to document this correction. All other sections preserved from iteration 2 as they passed validation.
